task: SMC-01: Verify seeker missile UI cleanup APIs

implementation_correctness.details: All three methods implement correct logic with proper edge case handling and error checking

test_coverage.details: All 187 tactical tests pass. No regressions detected. Tester validation confirms all three methods tested.

documentation_completeness.details: All six method declarations (three model, three GUI) have complete Doxygen comments with @brief, full description, @param, @return, @author, @date fields

code_quality.details: Code follows established seeker-subsystem patterns. No memory leaks, proper error handling, graceful edge case management.

model_gui_separation.details: FTacticalGame.h and .cpp have zero wx headers. FBattleScreen properly delegates with reDraw() only on state changes.

build_validation.details: Full build succeeds with no compilation errors or warnings. Both executables and test runners build successfully.

notes: deactivateActiveSeekerByID: Linear search through vector is consistent with existing activation patterns in the codebase
activateInactiveSeekerAtHex: Deterministic lowest-ID selection properly documented and implemented
getActiveSeekersByMovingPlayer: Read-only method correctly omits reDraw() call, consistent with getSeekerMissiles pattern
All three methods follow the same ownership filtering pattern using getMovingPlayerID()
FBattleScreen delegations properly check state change before calling reDraw()
Test coverage includes model-layer mechanics tests, GUI delegation tests, and integration tests
No circular dependencies or model-GUI boundary violations detected

recommendation: Ready for integration. All acceptance criteria satisfied. No blocking issues detected.
